Timing Values: I:E-Philosphy - Rate and I:E-Ratio


In this test, the applied rate and I:E-ratio is tested. For each partial test, the sequenze is:

(ASL)->(Setup)->(Exec)->(Scripting)->(Evaluation)->(CleanUp)

  • (ASL)-Block: Sets ASL resistance and compliance according to the product requirements.
  • (Setup)-Block: Sets predefined values according to the product requirements.
  • (Exec)-Block:
    1. Starts ventilation.
    2. Waits for 90 seconds.
    3. Measure I:E from the ventilator
    4. Stores one measurement of inspiration time and expiration time by the Flow-Analyser in the variables: [TI_FLOWANALYSER], [TE_FLOWANALYSER]
      5. . Note: The block takes two measurements from the ventilator, in case the first one is taken while the controller is auto-zeroing, resulting in an invalid measurment.
  • (Scripting)-Block:
    1. Checks the ventilator measurements from the (Exec)-block for invalid values and substitutes invalid values by an spare measurement, if necessary.
    2. Calculates the applied I:E-ratio measured by the ventilator, multiplies it by 100 and stores it in [I_E_VENTILATOR_FACTORED]
    3. Calculates the applied I:E-ratio measured by the flow-analyser, multiplies it by 100 and stores it in [I_E_FLOWANALYSER_FACTORED]
      4. Note: The calculated I:E-ratios are multiplied due to the insufficient capabilities of AIT to evaluate floating-point numbers.
  • (Evaluation)-Block:
    1. Evaluates the apllied I:E-ratio in [I_E_VENTILATOR_FACTORED]
    2. Evaluates the apllied I:E-ratio in [I_E_FLOWANALYSER_FACTORED]
    3. Evaluates the applied Rate measured by the ventilator
    4. Evaluates the applied Rate measured by the flow-analyser
  • (CleanUp)-Block: Stops ventilation.
